concept: Accommodate / Make a Particular Change for Access
description: To make a particular change in an environment, practice, timing, or form of support so a stated person can access or participate. The change answers an access relation, not a supposed diagnosis: 'hinolu' (adapt) can improve any fit, while 'hinawu' answers a person's stated access requirement.
sound_symbolism: Light 'hi' begins with the readiness heard in 'hinolu' (adapt). Grounded 'na' gives the change a particular need to answer, while open 'wu' leaves its form negotiable.
grammatical_notes: A transitive verb: 'lo mia ha phelona hinawu.' (We accommodate this requirement.) The event-noun rule supplies an accommodation. The object can be a requirement, activity, environment, or process, while the affected person may appear with 'wei'.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Shared spaces remain shared only when their arrangements can change for the people who use them.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: The verb makes a requested adjustment discussable without turning help into permission to control the person who asked.

tags.activity: changing a practice so a stated action becomes possible
tags.community: a particular response to an access requirement
tags.spatial: altering an environment or route for access
